violations: DispositionButtons.tsx:108-127 uses bare <button> for inline triage controls (Accept/Snooze/Dismiss) — ui-standards requires Button primitive, partial deduct on Primitive Reuse
RecRow.tsx — disposition state is local in DispositionButtons but row-level mutation doesn't propagate to QueueTable counts (state-drift risk; not a crash)
agent-activity 'live' surface is reduced to a static reasoning trail (RecDetail.tsx ActivityRow) — no SSE / live tile per agentic-principles event-spine-first; trade-offs.md openly accepts this
No live event-spine on home queue (manifest declares motion_budget: minimal and explicitly drops SSE)
strengths: rm_send_to_customer wired through the shared ApprovalGate primitive at /approval/[id] (SendApprovalClient.tsx — irrevocable contract honored)
Inline reversible disposition (Accept/Snooze/Dismiss) on every row — rm_disposition gate is unambiguous and sticky
Reuses 7 shared primitives by import from @fsi-bank/components (no inlined copies) — highest framework-fidelity of the three options
Deterministic mock data sourced from canvas-pinned _shared/mock-data.ts (Rule 4 respected)
Defensive lookups (getRec falls back to first pending; expiry math is null-safe)
Drill-in agent reasoning trail names atomic services + rule from the canvas (peer-and-industry-context, exposure-aggregator, regulatory-clearance)

violations: Inlines six shared primitives under components/primitives/ rather than importing from @fsi-bank/components — manifest declares source: shared and rationale.md acknowledges the trade, but byte-level it is a fork (drift risk; partial deduct on Primitive Reuse)
RightRail.tsx:62 uses inline style={{ width: `${pct}%` }} — acceptable for dynamic percentage, but no width token; borderline tokens-not-invention

Inline functions passed Server→Client at app/case/[id]/page.tsx (handlers live in DispositionClient — properly isolated, good — but the disposition prop is React.ReactNode bridged from Server which is OK)
No live event-spine surface (timeline is static rendering of rec.timeline); motion_budget: minimal acknowledged
strengths: Strongest narrative metaphor — process-as-primary-story is visible on every screen (customer story → proposal → impact → routes-to → evidence → ApprovalGate)
rm_disposition gate is the ApprovalGate primitive embedded INLINE in the NarrativeCard (case-detail) — gate placement is the page
rm_send_to_customer wired at /approval/[id] with explicit HITL roster + irrevocable flag passed to ApprovalGate
Evidence chips + agent learning panel + safety rails make audit-trail-as-SOP unusually concrete for a queue surface
RightRail surfaces the calibration loop (accept-as-drafted/with-edits/deferred/rejected over 30d + 'what the agent learned from your rejections') — strong human-in-the-loop signal
Self-contained build — Dockerfile builds standalone (Rule 38 honored)

violations: Inlines six shared primitives under primitives/ (via @primitives alias) — same fork-risk as B; manifest is explicit and post-build cleanup is wired
InlineDispositionRow.tsx:120-148 uses bare <button> for Accept/Reject/Snooze/Escalate — partial deduct on Primitive Reuse (should compose Button primitive once available)
QueueBoard recomputes counts on every render without memo — perf, not correctness
Per-row disposition state is local; no global queue state propagates filter counts after disposition change (filter-tab counts drift after Accept)
strengths: Cleanest reversible/irrevocable split — Accept/Reject/Snooze/Escalate inline (rm_disposition); /approval/[id] is the ONLY irrevocable surface, wraps shared ApprovalGate (rm_send_to_customer)
/case/[id] surfaces a dedicated TimelineList component reading every PIPELINE_EVENT — event-spine-first promise is concrete (each event → one row, nothing dropped)
GateRoster on /case/[id] explicitly enumerates both HITL gates with reversible/irrevocable badges — the user always knows where to act
Reject captures required short reason inline (override-rate analytics loop closed without breaking flow)
Snooze opens inline 24h/7d/30d picker — no modal, no popover; matches the affordance thesis
/approval page renders warning-tinted header + explicit 'irrevocable' badge — friction is proportional to the commit's cost
Server/Client boundary is correctly drawn (QueueBoard/InlineDispositionRow/SendToCustomerClient are 'use client'; pages and TimelineList/GateRoster are Server)

recommendation_rationale: Option C scores highest on both rubrics (composite 4.0). It has the cleanest reversible/irrevocable split — the four inline buttons on every queue card honor the rm_disposition gate at the lowest possible friction, and the rm_send_to_customer irrevocable gate routes to /approval/[id] wrapping the shared ApprovalGate with a warning-tinted header. It is the only option that ships a dedicated TimelineList component reading PIPELINE_EVENTS one-for-one (event-spine-first). The trade-off is bare <button> elements for the inline controls (partial Primitive Reuse deduct) and per-row state that drifts from filter-tab counts after disposition. Option B scores nearly as well on principles (4.2) but lower on standards (3.8) because it inlines six shared primitives as copies; the narrative metaphor is the strongest of the three but B is optimised for ~12 stories, not 50-200. Option A has the cleanest reuse (13 imports from @fsi-bank/components, no copies) and the densest triage surface, but explicitly drops live event-spine and the agent-reasoning trail is static. The human should pick C unless the use case in production needs the relationship-narrative reading mode B optimises for.
notes_for_human: Option D worktree was lost despite the Rule-46 attempt to recover it; this judge pass scored only A/B/C (missing_options: ['d']).
C and B are within 0.4 composite — if the production RMs are narrative readers (low queue volume, deep relationships) prefer B; if they are throughput-driven branch bankers (50-200/day) prefer C.
All three options correctly wire rm_send_to_customer through the shared ApprovalGate primitive at /approval/[id] — the irrevocable gate is honored across the board.
All three options inline disposition for rm_disposition (reversible) — but only C ships an explicit Reject-with-reason capture, which the canvas notes-block calls out as the model-feedback loop.

B and C inline shared primitives under components/primitives/ and primitives/ respectively (Rule 38 self-contained); A imports directly from @fsi-bank/components. The judge does not penalize this hard because manifests are explicit and provenance comments exist, but a follow-up audit should confirm byte-equivalence with the @fsi-bank/components versions to prevent fork drift.
Convergence is zero (largest Jaccard 0.33, A∩B) — designer diversity worked; the three options stake out genuinely different positions on density / metaphor / affordance.

Live event-spine is the weakest principle across the board — no option ships an SSE/in-flight agent tile. If 'agent activity visible live' matters for this use case, the winner should add a live-activity surface post-selection.
